commit.message:
feat: add RSVP change feed on event detail pages (#160)

* feat: add RSVP change feed on event detail pages

Add a GitHub Issue-style activity feed at the bottom of every event
page showing when people confirm or change their RSVP status.

Schema:
- New `rsvp_changes` table with eventId, userId, previousStatus
  (nullable for first RSVP), newStatus, and changedAt
- Indexes on eventId and (eventId, changedAt) for efficient feed queries
- CASCADE delete on both event and user foreign keys

Service layer:
- `recordRsvpChange()` — inserts a change record
- `getEventActivityFeed()` — returns feed entries with user names,
  ordered most recent first, with configurable limit (default 50)

Route changes:
- Action records RSVP changes for confirm/decline/attend/decline-attendance
  intents, with deduplication (skips if status hasn't actually changed)
- Loader includes activity feed data

UI:
- Activity section with timeline-style layout (border-left)
- First RSVP: "Tyler confirmed Going"
- Status change: "Tyler changed from Going → Not Going"
- Timestamps formatted with user's timezone preference

Tests:
- 8 new unit tests for recordRsvpChange and getEventActivityFeed
- 6 new integration tests covering recording, retrieval, ordering,
  limits, cascade delete, and event isolation
- Updated route test mocks for new exports
- Updated integration test helpers (seed factory, TRUNCATE list)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move activity feed timestamps to left side for vertical alignment

Reorder activity feed entry layout so timestamps appear first,
followed by a separator, then the user action text. This aligns
all timestamps on the left edge for better scanability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add E2E Playwright tests for RSVP change feed

Adds comprehensive end-to-end tests covering:
- Full RSVP lifecycle: performer confirm → decline → re-confirm
- Member self-registration via 'I'll be there' button
- Activity feed displays entries in reverse chronological order
- Deduplication: same-status submissions don't create duplicates
- Empty state: no activity section when no RSVP changes exist
- Feed persistence across page reloads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate activity feed entries on concurrent attend/decline

Move the dedup status check before DB writes in the attend and
decline-attendance action paths. Previously, concurrent requests
could both read stale previousStatus and create duplicate feed
entries. Now matches the early-return pattern used by confirm/decline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: extract ActivityFeed component with component explorer fixture

Extract the inline activity feed from the event detail route into a
reusable ActivityFeed component (app/components/activity-feed.tsx) and
add a component explorer fixture with four variants:

- Multiple Entries: confirmed, changed status, different users
- Empty Feed: renders nothing (null return)
- Single Entry: one initial RSVP
- Status Changes Only: users changing their RSVP

Moves STATUS_LABELS and formatStatusLabel into the component since
they are feed-specific. Removes unused History and formatDateTime
imports from the route.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: renumber orphaned migration 0012_high_gateway → 0013

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add OpenVINO base image with pre-cached IR model files (#2)

The BACKEND build arg controls which model format is cached:
- torch (default): PyTorch safetensors files
- openvino: OpenVINO IR files (openvino_model.xml + .bin)

The openvino variant installs optimum-intel + openvino and runs
SentenceTransformer(backend='openvino') at build time to download
the IR files from the HF repo's openvino/ folder.

CI matrix now builds both tags in parallel:
- 3.12-slim-multilingual-e5-base (torch)
- 3.12-slim-multilingual-e5-base-openvino (openvino IR)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: create reusable setup-go action to fix Go module cache in CI workflows (#8257)

Most CI workflows were using `actions/setup-go` without
`cache-dependency-path`, causing the action to scan for all `**/go.sum`
files in the repo. This produces an inconsistent cache key, leading to
frequent cache misses and modules being re-downloaded on every run.

## Changes

- Created a reusable composite action
`.github/actions/setup-go/action.yml` that wraps `actions/setup-go` and
always passes both `./go.sum` and `./internal/tools/go.sum` as
`cache-dependency-path`:

```yaml
- uses: actions/setup-go@...
  with:
    go-version: ${{ inputs.go-version }}
    cache-dependency-path: |
      ./go.sum
      ./internal/tools/go.sum
```

- Updated all 21 CI workflow files to use `./.github/actions/setup-go`
instead of calling `actions/setup-go` directly

The `check-go-version.sh` lint script continues to work correctly since
workflow files retain `go-version: 1.26.x` in their `with:` sections.
The `setup-go-tip` action is intentionally excluded — that workflow
explicitly purges the module cache immediately after setup.

commit.message:
Use gotestsum for all test execution (#8256)

Replaces the raw `go test | tee` pipeline and `bash -c "... | COLORIZE"`
patterns with [`gotestsum`](https://github.com/gotestyourself/gotestsum)
across all test targets for consistent formatting, automatic failure
reruns, and cleaner output.

## Changes

- **`internal/tools/tools.go`** — pins `gotest.tools/gotestsum v1.13.0`
as a tracked tool dependency
- **`scripts/makefiles/Tools.mk`** — adds `GOTESTSUM` variable; installs
it via `install-test-tools`
- **`Makefile`**:
- Adds `GOTESTSUM_FLAGS=--format pkgname-and-test-fails --format-icons
hivis` shared across targets
- `test`: replaces `bash -c "... go test -v ... | COLORIZE"` with
`gotestsum`
- `cover`: replaces `bash -c "... go test ... | tee"` with `gotestsum
--rerun-fails --packages ./...`
  - Removes `GOTEST`, `GOTEST_QUIET`, `COLORIZE` variables
- **`scripts/makefiles/IntegrationTests.mk`** — migrates
`all-in-one-integration-test`, `jaeger-v2-storage-integration-test`,
`storage-integration-test` to `gotestsum`

## Output format

```
✅  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics (2.28s)
➖  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics/benchmark (1.02s)
❌  github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/internal/metrics/foo (0.8s)
    --- FAIL: TestFoo (0.00s)

DONE 37 tests in 6.1s
```

- `pkgname-and-test-fails`: one line per package; failed test output
inlined
- `hivis`: high-visibility ✅ / ➖ / ❌ icons
- `--rerun-fails`: auto-reruns failing packages (up to 2×) in `cover`
- `--packages ./...` required alongside `--rerun-fails` per gotestsum's
API

* Fix CFG predict_step receiving batch_idx as guidance_scale (#53)

* Initial plan

* Fix CFG predict_step signature conflict with PyTorch Lightning

Both CFG models had predict_step(self, batch, guidance_scale=None) which
caused PyTorch Lightning to pass batch_idx as guidance_scale, silently
overriding the guidance scale set on the model. This resulted in:
- Wrong guidance scales being used during evaluation
- Nearly identical scores across different guidance strengths
- Worse scores compared to non-CFG models

Fix: Changed signature to predict_step(self, batch, batch_idx=None) and
read guidance_scale from self.guidance_scale attribute instead.

* Add permutation_importance_drn.py for DRN/EMOS models with copula postprocessing

DRN/EMOS models output distribution parameters (mu, sigma) rather than
samples directly. This script extends permutation importance to handle
the extra copula step:

1. Load model + data, run baseline forward pass
2. Convert distributional params to forecast samples via ECC or GCA
3. Compute baseline energy score on the copula samples
4. For each channel: inject PermuteChannel → forward pass → copula → ES
5. Record (ES_permuted - ES_baseline) / ES_baseline

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --split val --copula ecc -v

Reuses helpers from copulas_eval.py (stack_predictions,
predictions_to_dataarray, do_ecc, do_gca) and permutation_importance.py
(_build_x_transform, _get_channel_info, parse_device, log_msg).

* Add parallel launcher for DRN permutation importance

Discovers all input channels from cached metadata, launches one
permutation_importance_drn.py subprocess per channel in parallel.
Each subprocess writes to a unique temp CSV to avoid write collisions.
Results are merged into a single sorted output file once all finish.

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.launch_permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --max-parallel 4 --device 0 -v

Features:
- --max-parallel flag to limit concurrent subprocesses
- --channels to subset channels (forwarded to each subprocess)
- Per-channel temp files prevent write collisions
- Merged CSV sorted by importance descending

* Fix NaN in spatial normalization: clamp variance and std in _compute_tensor_stats

Two sources of NaN in spatial mode:
1. Negative variance from E[x²]-E[x]² numerical cancellation → sqrt(negative) = NaN
2. Zero std at constant grid cells → division by zero = Inf/NaN in __getitem__

Fix: clamp variance to min=0 before sqrt, clamp std to min=1e-6 in spatial mode.
This prevents NaN in norm_stats which was propagating to all_vars_mean and all_vars_std.

Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Identity credential IntelliSense (#57483)

* Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Identity credential IntelliSense

Add JSON schema to provide IntelliSense for credential configuration in
appsettings.json files. Defines credential sources (AzureCliCredential,
ManagedIdentityCredential, WorkloadIdentityCredential, etc.) with
conditional properties per credential type using draft-07 if/then patterns.

Includes secret-blocking patterns for ClientSecret, ClientCertificatePassword,
and Password to prevent secrets from being placed in configuration files.

Closes #56781

commit.message:
Move param type conversion util out of tracestore API into v1adapter (#8255)

`internal/storage/v2/api/tracestore/reader.go` imported
`internal/storage/v1/api/spanstore` solely to support the
`ToSpanStoreQueryParameters()` conversion helper — an undesirable v1
dependency in a v2 API package.

## Changes

- **`tracestore/reader.go`**: Remove `ToSpanStoreQueryParameters()`
method from `TraceQueryParams` and drop `spanstore`/`jptrace` imports.
The v2 tracestore API package is now free of v1 dependencies.
- **`v1adapter/tracereader.go`**: Add `GetV1QueryParameters(query
tracestore.TraceQueryParams) *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters` as an
exported function. The v1adapter package is the correct home for v1↔v2
conversion logic.
- **Tests**: Move `TestToSpanStoreQueryParameters` →
`TestGetV1QueryParameters` into `v1adapter/tracereader_test.go`.

```go
// Before (in tracestore package — wrong layer):
func (t *TraceQueryParams) ToSpanStoreQueryParameters() *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters { ... }

// After (in v1adapter package — correct layer):
func GetV1QueryParameters(query tracestore.TraceQueryParams) *spanstore.TraceQueryParameters { ... }
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* implement stream seek by required length

* improve tests

* fix build

* Refactor magic bytes mapping to use canonical extensions

Replaces multi-extension keys and MagicBytesSignatureEntry with a FrozenDictionary mapping canonical file extensions to their magic byte signatures. Introduces an ExtensionAliases map to resolve extension variants (e.g., .jpg → .jpeg). Updates signature lookup logic to use canonical forms, improving performance, maintainability, and extension handling. Removes the static constructor and legacy record.

* Refactor: use collection expressions for static dictionaries

Refactored `ExtensionAliases` and `ByFileExtension` to use C# collection expressions and indexer initializers, replacing the older object initializer syntax. This update improves code readability and leverages modern C# features without changing any logic or data.

* Improve SVG magic bytes validation with closing tag check

Enhanced SVG file detection by adding magic bytes signatures
that verify the file ends with valid "</svg>" closing tags,
in addition to checking for the opening "<svg" tag. This
ensures more accurate validation of SVG files.

* Rename ct to cancellationToken in IMagicBytesValidator

Renamed the cancellation token parameter from ct to cancellationToken in both the IMagicBytesValidator interface and its implementation for clarity and consistency. Updated XML documentation comments and method calls accordingly. Explicitly set the default value for the cancellation token parameter.

* Refactor MagicBytesValidator for clarity and consistency

Renamed private helper methods' parameters for better readability (e.g., buf → buffer, sig → signature) and changed Evaluate to EvaluateSignature with reordered parameters. Updated all internal usages accordingly. Also renamed the buffer variable in the main validation loop to magicBytes. No functional changes; this is a cosmetic refactor to improve code clarity and maintainability.

* Refactor magic byte checks; unify .heic signature logic

Refactor helper methods to use explicit block bodies and early returns for clarity. Update .heic magic bytes to use a single Offset check with multiple candidates, replacing separate checks for each possible signature.

* change samples images, add throw and logging in validation

* minore

commit.message:
fix(gallery): suppress NG0913 oversized image warning via IMAGE_CONFIG

External images shown in the gallery are user-supplied URLs at unknown
intrinsic dimensions. Rendering them at thumbnail/fixed heights will
always trigger NG0913. IMAGE_CONFIG with disableImageSizeWarning: true
is the documented Angular approach for use cases where image dimensions
cannot be controlled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"

Fix item access type inference (#280)

* Infer element types for ItemAccessExpression

Previously myList{0} always returned Type.Any. Now inspects the collection
type and returns:
- DefinedList: union of element types
- DefinedTable: DefinedRecord matching table columns
- Plain List: AnyInstance (unknown elements)
- Plain Table: RecordInstance
- AnyUnion: recursively resolves from union members
- Optional access (?): same type but nullable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify nullable test assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix 3 type inference bugs: range nullable, error type, null coalescing

B3: Range expression with nullable operands now returns List instead of None.
    Nullability is a runtime concern, not a type error.

B4: ErrorRaisingExpression now returns None instead of Any.
    Error expressions never produce a value  they always throw.

B5: Null coalescing (x ?? y) now strips nullability from left operand
    in the result union, since ?? guarantees non-null from left side.
